risks.description: Material grade is not legible/provided, so machinability cannot be confirmed against the shop's listed material capabilities.
risks.mitigation: Confirm material specification; provisional assessment assumes a common automotive machinable material such as steel or aluminum.

risks.description: The part is described as a complex cast housing with ribs, draft, pockets/windows, and raised lettering, but the shop profile does not list in-house casting/foundry capability.
risks.mitigation: Confirm whether casting is customer-supplied or allow outside casting/foundry sourcing before in-house machining.

risks.description: Many dimensions have no explicit tolerance and the title-block general tolerance standard is missing.
risks.mitigation: Confirm the drawing general tolerance standard before final feasibility/routing decisions.

risks.description: Several profile/position controls are achievable by machining tolerance level, but in-house inspection profile says no CMM in-house.
risks.mitigation: Use outside CMM/FAI for datum-based profile and position verification, especially first article inspection.

risks.description: The drawing calls for Zn-Ni coating/thread-after-coating for insert nuts or threaded features, while the shop profile lists alkaline zinc plating but not explicitly zinc-nickel plating.
risks.mitigation: Confirm coating specification and whether in-house plating line is qualified for Zn-Ni; otherwise outsource Zn-Ni coating.

risks.description: Several through holes/slots are modeled through up to 143.6 mm part thickness; these are within machine travel but may require careful fixturing, two-sided access, or long tooling.
risks.mitigation: Confirm local wall thicknesses from the 3D model and plan inspection/fixturing accordingly in Phase 2.

outside_processes_needed.reason: Complex cast housing body is implied, but no in-house casting capability is listed.
Datum-based profile and position controls on a complex housing likely require CMM verification; in-house inspection profile states cmm_in_house: false.

outside_processes_needed.process: zinc-nickel coating qualification or outside Zn-Ni plating
outside_processes_needed.reason: Zn-Ni coating is called out, but shop profile only explicitly lists alkaline zinc plating.
outside_processes_needed.sequence_position: after insert/coating preparation as required by drawing

outside_processes_needed.reason: Drawing includes push-out, anti-rotate torque, durability, and high-load tests; shop has general test capability but the exact load/fixture capability is not specified.
outside_processes_needed.sequence_position: after manufacture/assembly as required by control plan

feasibility.assessment_notes: The 335.78 x 232.62 x 143.6 mm complex housing fits the listed VMC travel of 600 x 400 x 400 mm and the listed lathe envelope where rotary features are applicable. Explicit dimensional tolerances and GD&T values are generally loose to moderate relative to 0.02 mm careful machining capability, but material, default tolerances, casting source, coating qualification, and CMM inspection are significant open risks.

clarifications.question: Confirm the material grade/specification and condition from the title block or 3D model.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Material is required to confirm machinability, cutting method, coating compatibility, and whether heat treatment or special tooling is needed.
clarifications.suggested_default: Assume common automotive steel/aluminum only for provisional feasibility.

clarifications.question: Confirm the general tolerance standard/default tolerances for untoleranced dimensions.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Many dimensions have no explicit tolerance, so their feasibility cannot be fully assessed without the default standard.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the housing is supplied as a casting, requires this shop to source casting, or is intended to be machined from another form.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The shop profile does not list in-house foundry/casting capability, while the feature graph describes a complex cast housing with ribs and draft.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat casting as customer-supplied or outside-sourced until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Confirm M10 thread depth, blind/through status, insert/nut specification, and the 4X recess diameter associated with the 17.50 dimension.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Thread/recess completeness affects feasibility of insert installation, flushness, and thread-after-coating verification.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as insert/nut features with undefined depth until confirmed.

clarifications.question: Confirm exact GD&T values and datum references for the M10 pattern, Ø16.90 bores, Ø72.04 bore, Ø74 step, Ø30/Ø20 clevis features, and clevis holes.
clarifications.why_it_matters: Extracted FCFs are low-confidence and affect datum fixturing and CMM inspection requirements.

clarifications.question: Confirm coating standard and whether the required Zn-Ni coating is within the shop's alkaline zinc plating capability or must be outsourced.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The shop lists alkaline zinc plating but not explicitly Zn-Ni plating; thread-after-coating requirements depend on coating thickness/process.
clarifications.suggested_default: Assume outside Zn-Ni qualification if not confirmed in-house.

clarifications.question: Confirm abbreviations O/R, I/P, A/C, and DRFT MAX and whether those dimensions are finished, reference, casting, or draft controls.
clarifications.why_it_matters: These abbreviations affect tolerance interpretation for the central diameter stack and relief features.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat as reference/casting-control dimensions until confirmed.

assumptions.text: Because material is missing, provisional feasibility assumes a common machinable automotive material within the shop's listed material capabilities, not hardened exotic material.

Missing processes such as casting, Zn-Ni coating qualification, CMM FAI, and special testing may be outsourced because the routing policy does not require all processes to be in-house and an outside_vendor placeholder exists.
Untoleranced dimensions are treated as information-missing rather than blocked; if the eventual general tolerance is around 0.05 mm or looser, the shop's VMC/SPM/CNC capabilities should be adequate.
Through holes and slots with modeled 143.6 mm depth are assumed to be accessible from both +Z and -Z as indicated in the feature graph.

reasoning_summary: The part fits the listed machine envelopes and the explicit bore, slot, thread, and GD&T tolerances are generally within the shop's machining capability. The assessment is feasible with high risks because material, general tolerances, casting source, Zn-Ni coating qualification, CMM inspection, and several low-confidence drawing interpretations remain unresolved. No hard blocker is assigned because the missing/non-in-house processes appear outsourceable under the provided profile rather than prohibited.
